metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_439
metadata.comment:
Rationale: A knockout mouse line for PKA R1 beta is used to study its role in synaptic plasticity. It is shown that long term depression (LTD) at the schaffer collaterals is severely reduced in this mouse line. This demonstrates a role for R1 beta in LTD.

LTD is a postsynaptic process, requiring the endocytosis of AMPA receptors (Beattie et. al.). The exact mechanisms by which PKAs act in LTD is not understood, but requires phosphorylation of GluR1 (Man et.al.).. Experimental description: Generation of Mutant Mice.
The targeting of embryonic stem cells and the establishment of chimeras carrying the RIP3 mutation have been described(18). Three of the four REK2-derived cell lines and one of the four D3-derived cell lines that produced fertile chimeras contributed to the germline.Mouse genotypes were determined by Southern blot.
